text: Answer FOUR of the ten essay questions.
Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write your answers using the Inspera Scan Paper provided.
Candidates should not copy and paste images from any other documents.
Note: Inspera Scan question codes MUST be noted during the exam.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer FOUR of the ten essays.
content: What have modern advances in Cryo-EM, and the predictions of 3D structures from amino acid sequences, told us about GPCR structure? What are their limitations?
Compare and contrast the main ascending tactile and nociceptive pathways from the skin to the cortex. How can information carried by these pathways be modified?
Critically evaluate the evidence for ventral and dorsal streams in visual processing.
Discuss the role and mode of action of retinoic acid in specifying the antero-posterior axis of the neural tube.
Compare and contrast the transduction, encoding and processing of chemosensory signals in olfaction and taste.
To what extent can the function of motor system components be considered in isolation?
Discuss the properties of the NMDA receptor that make it an attractive candidate for development and learning-related plasticity in the brain.
Discuss mechanisms that contribute to the precision of connectivity during the development of neuronal (e.g., somatotopic) maps. Give examples to illustrate your answer.
How did the 'lateral hypothalamic syndrome', produced by electrolytic lesions, influence our understanding of motivated behaviour?
What is the evidence that the hippocampus is necessary for encoding relationships between stimuli?
